Package org.aavso.tools.vstar.scripting

Class VStarScriptingAPI

java.lang.Object
org.aavso.tools.vstar.scripting.VStarScriptingAPI
public class VStarScriptingAPI extends Object
This is VStar's scripting Application Programming Interface. An instance of this class will be passed to scripts. All methods are synchronised to ensure that only one API method is being called at a time.

    Load a dataset from the specified path, adding it to the existing dataset.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static VStarScriptingAPI getInstance()
      Return Singleton.

    • loadFromFile

      public void loadFromFile(String path)
      Load a dataset from the specified path. This is equivalent to "File -> New Star from File..."
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the file.

    • additiveLoadFromFile

      public void additiveLoadFromFile(String path)
      Load a dataset from the specified path, adding it to the existing dataset.
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the file.

    • loadFromURL

      public void loadFromURL(String path)
      Load a dataset from the specified URL.
      Parameters:
      url - The URL of the file.

    • additiveLoadFromURL

      public void additiveLoadFromURL(String path)
      Load a dataset from the specified path, adding it to the existing dataset.
      Parameters:
      url - The URL of the file.

    • loadFromFile

      public void loadFromFile(String pluginName, String path)
      Load a dataset from the specified path using the (possibly partial) plug-in name to identify the observation source plug-in to use. This is equivalent to plugin display name.
      Parameters:
      pluginName - The sub-string with which to match the plug-in name.
      path - The path to the file.

    • additiveLoadFromFile

      public void additiveLoadFromFile(String pluginName, String path)
      Load a dataset from the specified path, adding it to the existing dataset using the (possibly partial) plug-in name to identify the observation source plug-in to use. his is equivalent to plugin display name with the additive checkbox selected.
      Parameters:
      pluginName - The sub-string with which to match the plug-in name.
      path - The path to the file.

    • loadFromURL

      public void loadFromURL(String pluginName, String url)
      Load a dataset from the specified URL using the (possibly partial) plug-in name to identify the observation source plug-in to use.
      Parameters:
      pluginName - The sub-string with which to match the plug-in name.
      url - The URL.

    • additiveLoadFromURL

      public void additiveLoadFromURL(String pluginName, String url)
      Load a dataset from the specified URL, adding it to the existing dataset, using the (possibly partial) plug-in name to identify the observation source plug-in to use.
      Parameters:
      pluginName - The sub-string with which to match the plug-in name.
      url - The URL.

    • loadFromAID

      public void loadFromAID(String name, double minJD, double maxJD)
      Load a dataset from the AAVSO international database.
      Parameters:
      name - The name (not AUID) of the object.
      minJD - The minimum JD of the range to be loaded.
      maxJD - The maximum JD of the range to be loaded.

    • loadFromAID

      public void loadFromAID(String name, double minJD, double maxJD, String bands, String obscodes, boolean loadMinimalFields)
      Load a dataset from the AAVSO international database.
      Parameters:
      name - The name (not AUID) of the object.
      minJD - The minimum JD of the range to be loaded.
      maxJD - The maximum JD of the range to be loaded.
      bands - A short band name.
      loadMinimalFields - Load a minimal field subset?

    • additiveLoadFromAID

      public void additiveLoadFromAID(String name, double minJD, double maxJD)
      Load a dataset from the AAVSO international database, adding it to the existing dataset.
      Parameters:
      name - The name (not AUID) of the object.
      minJD - The minimum JD of the range to be loaded.
      maxJD - The maximum JD of the range to be loaded.

    • additiveLoadFromAID

      public void additiveLoadFromAID(String name, double minJD, double maxJD, String bands, String obscodes, boolean loadMinimalFields)
      Load a dataset from the AAVSO international database, adding it to the existing dataset.
      Parameters:
      name - The name (not AUID) of the object.
      minJD - The minimum JD of the range to be loaded.
      maxJD - The maximum JD of the range to be loaded.
      bands - A short band name.
      loadMinimalFields - Load a minimal field subset?

    • getStarInfo

      public StarInfo getStarInfo(String name)
      Return a StarInfo object for named object.
      Parameters:
      name - The name (not AUID) of the object.
      Returns:
      The StarInfo object.

    • saveObsList

      public void saveObsList(String path, String delimiter)
      Save the raw or phase plot dataset (according to current mode) to a file of rows of values separated by the specified delimiter.
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the file to save to (as a string).
      delimiter - The delimiter between data items.

    • saveMeanList

      public void saveMeanList(String path, String delimiter)
      Save the raw or phase plot mean list (according to current mode) to a file of rows of values separated by the specified delimiter.
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the file to save to (as a string).
      delimiter - The delimiter between data items.

    • saveModelList

      public void saveModelList(String path, String delimiter)
      Save the raw or phase plot model list (according to current mode) to a file of rows of values separated by the specified delimiter.
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the file to save to (as a string).
      delimiter - The delimiter between data items.

    • saveResidualList

      public void saveResidualList(String path, String delimiter)
      Save the raw or phase plot residual list (according to current mode) to a file of rows of values separated by the specified delimiter.
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the file to save to (as a string).
      delimiter - The delimiter between data items.

    • saveLightCurve

      public void saveLightCurve(String path, int width, int height)
      Save the light curve for the current view mode (raw or phase plot) to a PNG image file.
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the file to save to (as a string).
      width - The desired width of the image.
      height - The desired height of the image.

    • phasePlotMode

      public void phasePlotMode()
      Switch to phase plot mode. If no phase plot has been created yet, this will open the phase parameter dialog.

    • lightCurveMode

      public void lightCurveMode()
      Switch to phase raw (light curve) mode.

    • phasePlot

      public void phasePlot(double period, double epoch)
      Create a phase plot given period and epoch.
      Parameters:
      period - The period on which to fold the data.
      epoch - The epoch (first Julian Date) for the phase plot.

    • polyfit

      public void polyfit(String seriesName, double degree)
      Create a polynomial fit model given the specified series and polynomial degree.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - Name of series to which polynomial fit should be applied.
      degree - The required polynomial degree.

    • createModel

      public void createModel(String pluginName, String seriesName, Object[] params)
      Create a model given the specified partial plugin name, series and parameters.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - Name of series to which the model creation operation should be applied.
      params - Array of parameters; could be of any type.

    • dcdftPeriod

      public Double[] dcdftPeriod(String seriesName, double lowPeriod, double highPeriod, double resolution)
      Perform DCDFT period analysis with period range.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long form of the series name, e.g. V or Johnson V.
      lowPeriod - The low value of the period range to search in.
      highPeriod - The high value of the period range to search in.
      resolution - The resolution of the search over the range.
      Returns:
      An array of top-hits periods.

    • dcdftFrequency

      public Double[] dcdftFrequency(String seriesName, double lowFrequency, double highFrequency, double resolution)
      Perform DCDFT period analysis with frequency range.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long form of the series name, e.g. V or Johnson V.
      lowFrequency - The low value of the frequency range to search in.
      highPeriod - The high value of the frequency range to search in.
      resolution - The resolution of the search over the range.
      Returns:
      An array of top-hits frequencies.

    • dcdftStandardScan

      public Double[] dcdftStandardScan(String seriesName)
      Perform DCDFT period analysis with standard scan.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long form of the series name, e.g. V or Johnson V.
      Returns:
      An array of top-hits frequencies.

    • wwzPeriod

      public Double[][] wwzPeriod(String seriesName, double minPeriod, double maxPeriod, double periodStep, double decay, double timeDivisions)
      Perform WWZ time-frequency analysis with period range.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long form of the series name, e.g. V or Johnson V.
      minPeriod - The low value of the period range to search in.
      maxPeriod - The high value of the period range to search in.
      periodStep - The resolution of the search over the range.
      decay - The wavelet decay constant to use.
      timeDivisions - The number of time divisions to use.
      Returns:
      An array of top-hits periods; may be empty.

    • wwzFrequency

      public Double[][] wwzFrequency(String seriesName, double minFreq, double maxFreq, double freqStep, double decay, double timeDivisions)
      Perform WWZ time-frequency analysis with period range.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long form of the series name, e.g. V or Johnson V.
      minFreq - The low value of the frequency range to search in.
      maxFreq - The high value of the frequency range to search in.
      freqStep - The resolution of the search over the range.
      decay - The wavelet decay constant to use.
      timeDivisions - The number of time divisions to use.
      Returns:
      An array of top-hits frequencies; may be empty.

    • getError

      public String getError()
      Return the last error.
      Returns:
      The error string; may be null.

    • getWarning

      public String getWarning()
      Return the last warning.
      Returns:
      The warning string; may be null.

    • getSeries

      public String getSeries()
      Returns a comma-separated string of series names for the current dataset, including bands and synthetic series such as means, model, residuals.
      Returns:
      a comma-separated series names

    • getTimes

      public double[] getTimes(String seriesName)
      Returns the time values (e.g. JD, HJD) for the specified series.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long series name.
      Returns:
      An array of time values.

    • getPhases

      public double[] getPhases(String seriesName)
      Returns the (standard) phase values for the specified series.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long series name.
      Returns:
      An array of phase values.

    • getMags

      public double[] getMags(String seriesName)
      Returns the magnitude values for the specified series.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The short or long series name.
      Returns:
      An array of magnitude values.

    • makeVisible

      public void makeVisible(String seriesName)
      Makes the specified series in the current dataset visible. Calling this method more than one consecutive time with the same visibility value has no effect on the current visibility status of a series.
      Parameters:
      seriesName - The long name (e.g. "Johnson V" not "V") of the series to make visible.

    • scatter

      public void scatter(String name, String xTitle, String yTitle, double[] xs, double[] ys)
      Create a scatter plot in the named tab.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the dataset to create or add to.
      xTitle - The x axis title.
      yTitle - The y axis title.
      xs - The x values to plot.
      ys - The y values to plot.

    • pause

      public void pause(long millis)
      Pause for the specified number of milliseconds.
      Parameters:
      millis - The time to pause for.

    • exit

      public void exit()
      Exit VStar
